text.title: Django + Celery Background Jobs Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Task fires but the row it needs does not exist yet; intermittent 'object not found' errors.
antipattern.root-cause: Bare .delay() called inside @transaction.atomic instead of on_commit.
antipattern.fix: Wrap dispatch in transaction.on_commit(lambda: task.delay(...)) or use task.delay_on_commit if available.

antipattern.symptom: Duplicate charges on the same order after a transient broker error.
antipattern.root-cause: Task lacked idempotent-key + business_key check on entry.
antipattern.fix: Add `if already_processed(business_key): return existing_result`; rerun affected rows once cleaned.

antipattern.symptom: Downstream API goes flaky; thousands of retries land in the same 50ms window.
antipattern.fix: Switch to full jitter; cap max_retries; add a circuit breaker.

antipattern.symptom: Webhook task fails 5 times and disappears; nobody notices until customers complain.
antipattern.root-cause: No DLQ; max_retries hit and acked.
antipattern.fix: Add a DLQ + on-call alert; replay queue tool for the on-call to drain.
